message.text: software-development-plan: A Software Development Plan must be established and maintained

description.text: Create and maintain a Software Development Plan covering lifecycle activities, deliverables, and traceability

message.text: software-verification-plan: A Software Verification Plan must define verification activities and acceptance criteria

description.text: Document verification strategy including unit tests, integration tests, and acceptance criteria

message.text: software-requirements-specification: Software requirements must be documented and traceable to system requirements

description.text: Create a Software Requirements Specification (SRS) with traceable requirements

message.text: architecture-documentation: Software architecture must be documented showing subdivision into software items

description.text: Document software architecture with component diagrams and interface descriptions

message.text: detailed-design: Detailed design documentation is required for Class C software items

description.text: Create detailed design documents for each software unit including algorithms and data structures

message.text: maintenance-plan: A Software Maintenance Plan must be established

description.text: Establish a maintenance plan covering feedback handling, modification analysis, and release procedures

message.text: risk-analysis: Software risk analysis per ISO 14971 must be performed and documented

description.text: Perform and document risk analysis in accordance with ISO 14971, including hazard identification and risk control

message.text: configuration-management: A Configuration Management system must be established for all software items

description.text: Implement configuration management covering version control, change control, and baseline identification

message.text: risk-analysis: A risk analysis must identify threats and vulnerabilities to ePHI

description.text: Conduct and document a comprehensive risk analysis identifying threats, vulnerabilities, and likelihood of impact to ePHI

message.text: workforce-security-policy: Policies must ensure workforce members have appropriate access to ePHI

description.text: Document workforce security procedures including authorization, supervision, clearance, and termination procedures for ePHI access

message.text: information-access-management: Access to ePHI must be managed based on role and minimum necessary standard

description.text: Document access authorization policies implementing the minimum necessary standard for ePHI access

message.text: security-awareness-training: Security awareness and training program must be established for all workforce members

description.text: Establish a security awareness training program covering password management, log-in monitoring, and malicious software protection

message.text: security-incident-procedures: Procedures for identifying, responding to, and mitigating security incidents must be documented

description.text: Document security incident response procedures including identification, response, mitigation, and documentation of incidents and outcomes

message.text: contingency-plan: A contingency plan must address data backup, disaster recovery, and emergency operations

description.text: Document contingency plan including data backup plan, disaster recovery plan, emergency mode operation plan, and testing procedures

message.text: facility-access-controls: Policies must limit physical access to electronic information systems and facilities

description.text: Document facility access controls including contingency operations, facility security plan, access control and validation, and maintenance records

message.text: business-associate-agreements: Business Associate Agreements must be in place with all entities that handle ePHI

description.text: Ensure Business Associate Agreements are executed with all vendors, subcontractors, and partners who create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI

message.text: documentation-retention-policy: HIPAA-required documentation must be retained for at least six years

description.text: Establish a documentation retention policy ensuring all HIPAA-required policies, procedures, and records are retained for a minimum of six years

message.text: data-classification-policy: A data classification policy must define sensitivity levels and handling procedures

description.text: Define data classification levels (public, internal, confidential, restricted) with handling, storage, and disposal requirements for each

message.text: encryption-policy: Encryption standards must be defined for data at rest and in transit

description.text: Document encryption requirements specifying algorithms, key lengths, key management, and certificate lifecycle for data at rest and in transit
